questions.question: Which command displays the full commit history of a repository?
What does the --oneline flag do when used with git log?
options: Displays each commit on a single compact line
Removes the author information from output
Filters commits to one specific branch
questions.answer: Displays each commit on a single compact line

questions.question: Which flag shows the branch and merge structure visually in the log?
How do you limit git log output to the last 5 commits?
What information does the --stat flag add to git log output?
options: Which files changed and how many lines changed per commit
The total number of commits in the repository
CPU and memory usage of each commit operation
questions.answer: Which files changed and how many lines changed per commit

questions.question: What does the command git show <hash> display?
options: Only the files that were changed
The commit metadata and the full diff of what changed
A list of all branches containing that commit
questions.answer: The commit metadata and the full diff of what changed

questions.question: What does HEAD refer to in Git?
options: The first commit ever made in the repository
The most recent commit on the current branch
The main branch of the repository
questions.answer: The most recent commit on the current branch

questions.question: How many characters long is a full Git commit hash?
How many characters of a commit hash are typically enough to uniquely identify a commit?
What does the command git diff <hash1> <hash2> do?
options: Shows every change that happened between two commits
Lists all files that exist in both commits
questions.answer: Shows every change that happened between two commits

questions.question: What does HEAD~3 refer to?
options: The third file changed in HEAD
3 lines before HEAD in the diff

questions.question: What does git blame <file> show for each line?
options: Which line has the most errors
The commit hash, author, date, and line content for who last modified that line
A list of all files the author has changed
The total number of times each line was modified
questions.answer: The commit hash, author, date, and line content for who last modified that line

questions.question: Which command would you use to search commit messages for the word 'Fix'?
How do you view the log of commits that changed a specific file called src/Main.java?
What does the %h placeholder represent in a custom git log format?
What does the %an placeholder represent in a custom git log format?
What does the %ar placeholder display in a custom git log format?
options: The absolute date of the commit
The relative date such as '2 hours ago'
The number of files added or removed
questions.answer: The relative date such as '2 hours ago'

questions.question: What does the %s placeholder represent in a custom git log format?
options: The subject or first line of the commit message
The number of files in the snapshot
questions.answer: The subject or first line of the commit message

questions.question: Which combination of flags shows a decorated, graphical, one-line view of all branches?
What is git blame most useful for determining?
options: Who changed what and when in a specific file
Which files are the largest in the repository
How many branches contain a given file
questions.answer: Who changed what and when in a specific file

questions.question: Which command shows the details of the most recent commit without knowing its hash?
What type of string is a Git commit hash?
What does the %ae placeholder represent in a custom git log format?
Which shorthand also works to limit git log to the last 3 commits?
What four pieces of information does each entry in a standard git log show?
options: Branch, tag, remote, and merge status
File name, line count, additions, and deletions
Repository, directory, file, and function name
